target_claim: L-1444: orient.py requires all 127 tools (monolithic dependency graph). GAP-6: tool monolith prevents lightweight genesis.
expect: orient.py dependency fan-out >20 tools; lazy-import refactor reduces MVD to <20 files
actual: GAP-6 monolith claim PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. orient.py CORE dependencies: 11/128 tools (8.6%). OPTIONAL imports: 15 more (all wrapped in try/except, graceful degradation). Total fan-out: 26 tools, not 127. MVD file count: 29 (similar to L-1444's 30). MVD size: 0.6 MB (vs L-1444's 3 MB — 5x reduction). orient.py --coord mode produces useful output with only CORE deps in ~3s. The 'monolith' is actually a well-structured graceful-degradation pattern — each optional section fails silently. Bottleneck is NOT tool dependencies but STATE file content (beliefs, lessons, frontiers = bulk of daughter size).
diff: PREDICTION 1 (fan-out >20): CONFIRMED — 26 total dependencies, 11 required + 15 optional. PREDICTION 2 (MVD <20 files): FALSIFIED — 29 files needed for functional daughter (state files dominate, not tools). KEY SURPRISE: L-1444 '127 tools required' was wrong by 5-12x. Orient's import strategy is already correct (lazy/optional/wrapped). GAP-6 should be reclassified: the structural bottleneck is state file volume (beliefs+memory+tasks), not tool dependencies. Lightweight genesis requires state compaction, not tool refactoring.
